body.title: Cap ollama inputs at model context length before sending
body.proposal: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.description:
Igor regularly sends oversized inputs to ollama, producing repeated 'llm embedding error: the input length exceeds the context length' in the ollama service log (seen throughout 2026-04-27). These are silent failures on Igor's side but cause ollama to return errors and accumulate partial work. On 2026-04-27 a 46.5s/500 chat request from a swarm machine pushed memory over the OOM threshold and triggered the cascade that orphaned PID 237776 for 24+ hours.

Fix: add a hard character-based truncation before every ollama call — embeddings and chat both. Safe caps (4 chars/token estimate): nomic-embed-text 8192 tokens → 32000 chars; qwen2.5:7b 4096 tokens → 15000 chars. Log a WARNING when truncation fires so the oversized call site can be found and fixed upstream.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/embedder.py (embed() — truncate before POST), wild_igor/igor/cognition/reasoners/ollama_reasoner.py (chat path — truncate prompt before POST)
**Design rules:** docs-in-code (update Updated timestamp in both files). No new schemas.
**Scope boundary:** IN: truncation at the two ollama call sites with WARNING log. OUT: actual tokenizer integration, upstream call-site fixes (those are follow-ons once the WARNING surfaces them).
**Test plan:** Add unit tests: embed() with a 40000-char string should not raise; ollama_reasoner chat with a 20000-char prompt should truncate and log WARNING. Run: python -m pytest tests/ -x -q -k 'embed or ollama_reason'
